item.title: Netflix delays Greta Gerwig’s ‘Narnia’ movie for big theatrical push in 2027
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: "The Magician's Nephew" looks like a big next step in Netflix's thawing relationship with movie theaters.

item.title: The best AI dictation apps, tested and ranked
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: AI-powered dictation apps are useful for replying to emails, taking notes, and even coding through your voice

item.title: Beyond Lovable and Mistral: 21 European startups to watch
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: It is not that European startups never get attention — Lovable and Mistral AI are proof of that. But there are many more that insiders are tracking.

item.title: Uber wants to turn its millions of drivers into a sensor grid for self-driving companies
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Praveen Neppalli Naga, Uber's chief technology officer, revealed the plan in an interview at TechCrunch's StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, describing it as a natural extension of a nascent program the company announced in late January called AV Labs.

item.title: Replit’s Amjad Masad on the Cursor deal, fighting Apple, and why he’d rather not sell
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: At TechCrunch's sold-out StrictlyVC event in San Francisco on Thursday night, we covered a lot of ground in a short time, beginning with the question everyone in the industry is asking right now: in a world where rival Cursor is reportedly in talks to be acquired by SpaceX for $60 billion, is Replit also bound to sell?

item.title: Musely secures $360M from General Catalyst without giving up equity
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The DTC skin, hair, and menopause care brand will use the non-dilutive capital to super-charge customer acquisition.

item.title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Meta bought humanoid startup Assured Robot Intelligence to beef up its AI models for robots, the company said.

item.title: Coatue has a plan to buy up land for data centers, possibly for Anthropic
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Coatue, one of the biggest names in venture capital, has a new venture that is reportedly buying land near large power sources.

item.title: Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
item.dc:creator: Ram Iyer
item.description: The deals come as the DOD has doubled down on diversifying its exposure to AI vendors in the wake of its controversial dispute with Anthropic over usage terms of its AI models.

item.title: Ubuntu services hit by outages after DDoS attack
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: A group of hacktivists have claimed responsibility for a distributed denial-of-service attack, which has affected several Ubuntu and Canonical websites, and prevented users from updating the Linux-based operating system.

item.title: Musk v. Altman is just getting started
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Elon Musk spent the better part of three days on the witness stand this week in his lawsuit against OpenAI, and it’s already getting messy. Emails, texts, and his own tweets are surfacing in court, and there are plenty more witnesses to come. Musk’s argument against OpenAI? By converting the company to a for-profit model, Sam Altman betrayed the “nonprofit for the […]

item.title: People are finally using Reddit’s search
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The company saw a 30% year-on-year jump in the number of people using search every week, CEO Steve Huffman said on Thursday.

item.title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 is a hit in India, but not a big winner elsewhere, yet
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Users in India are embracing ChatGPT Images 2.0 for creative, personal visuals — from avatars to cinematic portraits.

item.title: As Tim Cook steps down, Apple hit record sales — but a chip shortage looms
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Cook warned that Apple is facing supply-chain headwinds from RAMageddon that could impact its business.

item.title: Y Combinator alum Skio sells for $105M cash, only raised $8M, founder says
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Subscription billing fintech Skio sold to its competitor Recharge in what was a healthy exit, according to its founder and former CEO.

item.title: Sources: Anthropic potential $900B+ valuation round could happen within 2 weeks
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: Anthropic is asking investors to submit allocations for the AI company’s latest fundraise within the next 48 hours, according to sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: Apple was surprised by AI-driven demand for Macs
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple said it will be supply-constrained on Mac mini, Studio, and Neo in the next quarter, too.

item.title: Legal AI startup Legora hits $5.6B valuation and its battle with Harvey just got hotter
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: The two wildly fast-growing rivals have raised massive sums, pushed into each other's home turf, and now have dueling ad campaigns.

item.title: Rivian downsizes DOE loan to $4.5B for Georgia factory
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane, Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Rivian has reworked its loan deal with the Department of Energy and now expects to borrow $4.5 billion to build its new factory in Georgia, down from the original amount of $6.6 billion.

item.title: Hackers are actively exploiting a bug in cPanel, used by millions of websites
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Web hosts are scrambling to fix the bug under active attack by hackers. One company said hackers have been abusing the bug for months.
